note: Single coin, numeral 50 clearly readable on zoom. Strong bluish color cast (gold tone not visible).

Seed 0 shows numeral 5 + CENT + globe. Seed 2 shows numeral 1 + globe. Seed 1 face is worn/unreadable even after denoise+CLAHE; sized between seed 2 (1c) and seed 0 (5c), ratio to 5c ~0.84-0.88, so 2cent by relative size, copper family assumed. Bluish cast, oblique view.

Very dark, nearly illegible coin. After denoise/contrast stretch a large oval/disc relief is visible lower-right (globe of 1/2/5c common face, or Colosseum of Italian 5c national side); both readings point to copper family, guessed 5cent. Low confidence.
Bimetallic, big numeral 2 with map; outer ring is the SILVER ring (2euro: silver ring, gold center). Colors degraded teal/pink but two-tone ring visible.

Seed 0 reads 20 EURO CENT clearly. Detector missed the upper coin: numeral 5 + CENT + globe clearly readable (extra, Hough-fit cx=413 cy=394 r=80 in original coords). Strong bluish cast on both coins.

Asphalt/stone background. Seed 0: 5 EURO CENT, copper-pink, slightly overlapped by neighbors but circle fits. Seed 1: numeral 10, greenish-degraded gold. Seed 2: 20 EURO CENT. Relative radii (10c:5c:20c = 91:98:103) match real diameters well.
